steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: The previous sentence says slow blinks made speakers talk for less time. The blank introduces a sentence saying quicker blinks were associated with longer talking times. These two outcomes are opposite: slow blinks = less talk, quick blinks = more talk. The transition must signal contrast or reversal.

steps.title: Eliminate each wrong choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘For example,’: Signals that the second sentence illustrates the first — but the second sentence contradicts the first, not exemplifies it. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Specifically,’: Signals that the second sentence adds detail to the first — but it introduces an opposite finding, not a clarification. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Firstly,’: Signals a first item in a sequence — but this sentence is not the first point; the previous sentence already gave a finding. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘By contrast,’: Signals opposition between two ideas — exactly right, since slow blinks and quick blinks produced opposite talking durations. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
